videos.title: Back from a much needed staycation hence the delay in March’s best in...
videos.description: Back from a much needed staycation hence the delay in March’s best in media 🤭 To be honest the most important piece here is Severance. I spent far too much time looking up theories and watching behind the scenes footage because I just couldn’t get enough. Some of youz said you couldn’t get in to Severance, and I understand it’s a slow burn but trust me, stick with it. It has solidified itself with television greats like Breaking Bad, Mad Men, Game of Thrones, Big Little Lies. I cannot rave about it enough! March’s movies were all over the place. I went through a historical-romance phase. I also didn’t watch as many because as I mentioned before, I was far too invested in Severance. I read All My Rage by Sabaa Tahir in a single day. I couldn’t put it down. The characters are going to stick with me for a while. Ariana Grande’s deluxe album is so obviously her best work. The growth, lyricism, and cohesive storytelling throughout the album is perfection. Gaga’s much awaited Mayhem has certified bops, but I must admit I don’t find it cohesive and I am more Chromatica-leaning as a listener. Still one of my all time fav pop stars forever. . . . #bestofmarch #popculture #severance #whattowatch #arianagrande #ladygaga #movies #tv #film #whattoread #sabaatahir

videos.title: The shortest month bids adieu and with that comes the standouts of my...
videos.description: The shortest month bids adieu and with that comes the standouts of my media consumption for the month. Severance has solidified itself on the Mount Rushmore of greatest tv shows of all time. Each episode is better than the last, making it well worth the 3 year wait between seasons. Jeremy Strong and Sebastian Stan gave stellar performances in The Apprentice. It’s no wonder 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑚𝑎𝑛 hates the film and gave it a scathing review. I personally think Jeremy Strong deserves the Oscar for this one. I also revisited some of my favorite romances from the 90s. 1995 was a sweet year for that! I finally read a book that didn’t make me scoff- The Unmaking of June Farrow. It was unexpected and left me thinking well after I finished it. Also honorable mention for the short story The Sixth Deaths of the Saint. Just 31 pages yet it left a mark on me. Say what you will about Lisa Rinna but she is at her best with her husband Harry Hamlin, simply talking about their relationship (but breaking glasses in Amsterdam is still her Mt. Everest). I skipped video essays and music for this month because nothing rocked my world enough to mention here. Maybe something will come to mind later? Have a wonderful March! #whattowatch #movies #booktok #popculture

videos.title: Every January we complain about the endless length of this month, and...
videos.description: Every January we complain about the endless length of this month, and this January was no exception. In its 87 days we saw a lot of rock bottom days, and I logged everything I watched/read/learned and I’m sharing my picks with you now. Sofia Coppola kept her reigning position as my favorite director. No one can capture the femininity and complexity of girlhood like she can, and she has proven it time and time again with every single piece of work she puts out. Freida McFadden disappointed me in one book and thrilled me in the next. Severance returned after a grueling 3 year wait with some of the most beautiful shots in television history. Flawless writing. Empire of the Sun gave me another album to play when I’m handed the AUX. The Weeknd’s “Hurry Up Tomorrow” released today and it was an immediate masterpiece. Sunset Boulevard reignited the fire that burns within me for movies about movies. I immediately dove into YouTube to dissect Sunset Boulevard’s mastery, the best video essay is listed above. Nosferatu was a feast for the eyes, despite the thousands of rats. The entire cast deserves award recognition beyond all the valid critical acclaim. ♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎ I just started this a couple of months ago and it really helps me capture where I was personally by reassessing what I leaned towards. I love to visit old and new; revisit things I’ve already seen or read to see how older, wiser me will take it in. ♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎♡︎ And then there's you- this small space of people on the internet l've made a connection with that love to talk about this stuff with me. I only have a tiny group of people IRL that ~get this so to find people here has been like sunshine. ☀️ I’m also logging everything on Letterboxd and Goodreads, so if you’re on there let’s connect! And as always, I’m open to suggestions! X♡︎X♡︎ . . . . #popculture #film #tv #books #bookstagram #cinema #movies #letterboxd #goodreads #whattowatch #letterboxd #january #bestofthemonth

videos.title: Only a singular book was read this month because frankly, I’ve decide...
videos.description: Only a singular book was read this month because frankly, I’ve decided to embrace full brain rot and consume as many TikToks as possible before the app is snatched from us. As far as films go, Angelina Jolie knocked my socks off in Maria. By far Pablo Larrain’s best work in comparison to his previous films centered on historically influential women, Jackie (2016) and Spencer (2021). Additionally, I went in to Conclave blind and it was a masterpiece- a slow and steady build that crescendoed into a gossipy mess akin to any of the Real Housewives franchises. There are whispers that Conclave may be the surprise Best Picture winner? If that’s the case, I genuinely would understand. Earlier this month I shared my picks for best documentaries of 2024, but at the time I had not yet seen Meg’s documentary. It is easily in my favorites of the year. Her transparent window in to her mental state during the highest and lowest points of her life struck a chord with me, and as different of a life I live from her (I am not today’s greatest female rapper) I found her to be extremely relatable. That specific tone of anxiety is something I feel so deeply, and my respect for her only grew more. If you read up until this point I appreciate you, and as always- I’m open to suggestions! Happy New Year! I love you so much 🩷 #popculture #movies #tv #filmtok #whattowatch
